title: Implement open-source readiness phase 1
description: Implement the initial public-readiness baseline for the repository: public-safe docs, security and conduct policies, contributor templates, safe GitHub workflows, and immediate GitHub repo security settings. This issue covers the work that can be truthfully implemented in the current scaffold and excludes maturity-dependent controls that will be tracked separately.
design: Keep the repo honest about its current maturity. Add only controls that are meaningful now, avoid fake build/test guarantees, and route security reports to GitHub private reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Repository includes SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CODEOWNERS, issue/PR templates, safe PR and release workflows, and Dependabot config; README/CONTRIBUTING/maintainer docs are updated for a pre-code public scaffold; GitHub main branch protection and relevant security settings are enabled where available; deferred readiness items are filed as separate beads issues.
notes: Addressed review follow-up: narrowed CODEOWNERS to sensitive paths only, removed latest-release suppression from the release workflow, and updated branch protection so code-owner review enforcement is disabled until a second maintainer exists. Validation rerun: git diff --check, YAML parse, doc-path check, branch protection verification.
close_reason: Closed

title: Beads smoke test
close_reason: Embedded Beads setup, bootstrap flow, and minimal maintainer docs are complete

title: Run isolated agent eval variants for OpenHealth skill
description: Run the realistic agent evals documented in docs/agent-evals.md using isolated skill or harness variants for the generated-client baseline, production code-first SDK path, and any future CLI-oriented baseline. Capture tool calls, assistant calls, wall time, token use, correctness, duplicate behavior, and whether agents inspect generated files or module cache.
close_reason: Implemented isolated Codex eval harness, ran production SDK and generated-client variants, reported results in docs/agent-eval-results/oh-5yr-2026-04-16.md, and filed follow-ups oh-vul and oh-w4n.

title: Add code-first agent weight SDK surface
description: Implement an ergonomic code-first OpenHealth weight surface for agents, add idempotent duplicate handling, update the production openhealth skill to the blessed Go path, document realistic eval variants outside the skill, and fix install docs that reference unpublished tags.
close_reason: Addressed review finding by adding a storage-level unique index for active manual weight date/unit rows, mapping duplicate insert violations to conflicts, and rerunning quality gates.

title: Honor explicit weight-history database path before resolving defaults
description: Address review feedback for examples/openhealth/weight_history so the helper parses -db before resolving the default local database path.
close_reason: Parsed -db before resolving the default path and verified the helper/tests.

title: Improve OpenHealth weight history skill usability
description: Add skill documentation and a focused example so agents can answer local OpenHealth weight-history questions without rediscovering database path resolution or generated-client boilerplate.
close_reason: Implemented weight-history skill docs and focused helper example; validation and tests passed.

title: Make skill validator YAML-aware
description: Address review feedback on scripts/validate-agent-skill.sh by parsing SKILL.md frontmatter as real YAML so the CI gate accepts valid Agent Skills metadata and rejects malformed frontmatter.

title: Make openhealth skill spec-compatible and CI-guarded
description: Bring skills/openhealth/SKILL.md into compliance with the Agent Skills specification by adding required frontmatter, keeping the body concise and portable, and adding a repo-local validation check wired into pull request policy checks so future edits cannot drift out of spec.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix versioned Go install command in SDK docs
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done\n\nThe new SDK-first docs currently tell users to run go get github.com/yazanabuashour/openhealth/client@v0.1.0. That fails for the current module layout because client is a package inside the root module, not a separately versioned module. Update the install instructions to use the root module path for version selection while keeping github.com/yazanabuashour/openhealth/client as the package users import in code.
close_reason: Updated the SDK docs to use the root module path for versioned go get commands and aligned the external-consumer smoke test with that install flow.

title: Publish first public v0.1.0 source release
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done\n\nThe install story and SDK-facing docs are now aligned around github.com/yazanabuashour/openhealth/client and the in-process local runtime. Remaining work is the actual first public release: tag v0.1.0, push the tag, let the release workflow publish the source archive, checksums, SPDX SBOM, and attestations, then verify that consumers can resolve github.com/yazanabuashour/openhealth/client@v0.1.0 as a real semver release instead of a pseudo-version.

title: Simplify OpenHealth install story around Go SDK
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done\n\nOpenHealth already has an in-process local runtime via client.OpenLocal(...), but the public install story still reads like a repo/maintainer setup and there are no published semver tags. This work should make the Go SDK install path the primary end-user surface, keep the standalone HTTP server as maintainer/debug-only, remove tool-only dependencies from the consumer-facing module graph, add an external consumer smoke test, and update release/docs wording for the first v0.1.0 source release contract.
close_reason: Completed the Go SDK-first install-story work: docs now point consumers at github.com/yazanabuashour/openhealth/client and client.OpenLocal(...), tool-only module requirements were removed, and an external consumer smoke test now verifies the no-daemon install path from a fresh module.

title: Configure GitHub release environment protections
description: The release workflow now targets a dedicated GitHub `release` environment with scoped permissions, but the repository settings still need manual protection rules. Configure the `release` environment in GitHub so publish jobs require the intended approval path and remain isolated from routine CI.
notes: Configured in GitHub on 2026-04-15: created the release environment, required reviewer yazanabuashour, self-review allowed for the single-maintainer setup, and a custom deployment policy that allows only v* tags.
close_reason: Configured the GitHub release environment via the GitHub API: created the protected  environment, added yazanabuashour as the required reviewer with self-review allowed for the single-maintainer setup, and added a custom deployment policy allowing only  tags.

title: Stabilize PR queue and merge open PRs
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
Stabilize the current open pull request queue by merging the passing dependency bump PRs, updating the feature branch on top of the refreshed main branch, fixing the dependency-review failure on PR #4, and then syncing and cleaning local branches.

Acceptance criteria:
- PR #2 is merged with passing checks
- PR #3 is merged with passing checks
- PR #4 is updated, passes Policy checks, Go checks, and Dependency review, and is merged
- local main is refreshed from origin and merged local branches are deleted
- session stops at manual review per repo workflow
notes: Merged PR #2 and PR #3. Rebased PR #4 onto refreshed main, kept the workflow action bumps plus generated-code drift check intact, remediated GHSA-p77j-4mvh-x3m3 by upgrading google.golang.org/grpc to v1.79.3, and pushed commit ea23741. PR #4 now has green Policy checks, Go checks, and Dependency review, but remains blocked on external review because GitHub will not allow self-approval.
close_reason: Closed

title: Plan production deployment for the OpenHealth service
description: The initial OpenAPI-first health service and generated Go client are implemented locally, but the repo still needs a concrete production deployment workflow, environment contract, and operational guidance for running the SQLite-backed service outside local development.
close_reason: Implemented the supported local runtime contract: the generated client now exposes an in-process local bootstrap, SQLite defaults to ~/.local/share/openhealth, the maintainer CLI uses the same storage default, and the README/example/SKILL guide the no-daemon install story.

title: Implement OpenAPI-first health API and generated Go client
description: Build the initial openhealth v1 health service around an OpenAPI source of truth, generated Go client/server bindings, SQLite-backed repositories, and strict contract tests modeled after bespoke's health architecture without auth or multi-user support.
notes: Addressed review finding: baseline legacy SQLite schemas without schema_migrations and added migrator coverage for full and partial legacy schemas.

title: Bootstrap first Go module and toolchain
description: Initialize the repository's first runnable Go component with repo-managed Go tooling. The work should add a minimal Go module and executable entrypoint, wire in local setup and CI checks that actually validate the Go surface, and keep docs honest about the new runtime contract.
acceptance_criteria: Repository contains a runnable Go module and executable entrypoint; maintainer tooling installs the required Go toolchain and lint/test tooling; contributor docs and pull-request checks include truthful setup, run, format, lint, and test commands for the Go surface.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scaffold openclerk and openbudget repository baselines
description: Create the initial public-safe scaffolds for the openclerk and openbudget repositories using openhealth as the baseline. This includes copying the documentation and GitHub policy/workflow surface with repo-specific substitutions, initializing Beads in embedded mode with explicit prefixes, seeding the deferred readiness backlog, and creating the corresponding public GitHub repositories under yazanabuashour.
notes: Local scaffolds for openclerk and openbudget are complete. Both GitHub repositories have been created, Beads has been initialized in embedded mode with explicit prefixes, the six deferred readiness issues have been seeded in each repo, and each repo has a local scaffold commit. Paused before git push for manual review per workflow.

title: Validate and apply current Dependabot GitHub Actions recommendation
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done\n\nDependabot opened a valid PR to bump actions/checkout in the pull request workflow. This task verifies the recommendation is still green, fixes the missing repository label expected by dependabot.yml, and merges the valid PR without expanding scope beyond current repo-specific Dependabot work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add deployment and publishing trust boundaries when deploys exist
description: If the project introduces deployment targets or package publishing, establish separate trust boundaries for build, test, and release. Move to short-lived OIDC credentials, protected environments, and tight workflow permissions for tags, releases, packages, and deployments.
acceptance_criteria: Release and deploy workflows use protected environments where applicable, avoid long-lived secrets, and scope write permissions only to the workflows that need them.
notes: Workflow hardening is implemented in .github/workflows/release.yml: validation is separated from publish, publish runs in the GitHub release environment, permissions are scoped to contents/id-token/attestations write on the publish job, and no long-lived release secrets are required. Remaining repo-admin environment protection setup is tracked in oh-9s9.
close_reason: Completed release trust-boundary hardening. The release workflow uses scoped contents/id-token/attestations permissions and the dedicated release environment is now configured in GitHub with required reviewer protection and a custom deployment policy limited to v* tags.

title: Harden releases when the first distributable artifact exists
description: Once the project ships a distributable artifact, strengthen release authenticity and supply-chain evidence. This includes signed tags or artifacts, provenance, SBOM publication, checksums, and making the build reproducible enough to verify release integrity.
acceptance_criteria: Release process includes signing or attestations appropriate to the artifact type, publishes an SBOM and checksums when relevant, and documents how users can verify release integrity.
close_reason: Release hardening is implemented in .github/workflows/release.yml and docs/release-verification.md: tagged releases now validate before publish, attach a canonical source archive plus SHA256 checksums and SPDX SBOM, and generate GitHub provenance and SBOM attestations for release verification.

title: Open-source readiness backlog
description: Track readiness items that should not be implemented until the repository grows beyond the current scaffold stage. This issue groups deferred work around real runtime checks, stronger release integrity, deployment trust boundaries, maintainer isolation, and ongoing security operations.

title: Add real contributor gates when the first runnable package or app lands
description: When the repository gains its first runnable package or application, add truthful contributor gates tied to that surface. This includes real test commands, local run instructions, compatibility expectations, support policy details, and language-appropriate code scanning or linting.
acceptance_criteria: Contributor docs include real setup and validation commands; CI enforces the actual checks needed for the first runnable component; support and compatibility expectations match the shipped surface.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add required manual review gate to AGENTS workflow
description: Update AGENTS.md session completion instructions to add a required Manual review step after issue status updates and before push, with clear pause/resume wording that preserves push as mandatory for completion.
close_reason: Closed

title: Allow manual review hold in AGENTS session workflow
description: Update AGENTS.md session completion workflow so a user-requested manual code review hold is allowed after quality gates and issue closure, while keeping push mandatory after approval.
close_reason: Closed

title: Trim README and CONTRIBUTING; add maintainer docs
description: The root docs currently mix contributor-facing guidance with maintainer-only Beads operational details. Trim README.md to a public entry point, keep CONTRIBUTING.md focused on contribution expectations and Beads policy boundaries, and move maintainer setup/sync notes into docs/maintainers.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add runnable CLI-oriented agent eval variant
description: oh-5yr reported the CLI-oriented eval variant as not runnable because cmd/openhealth currently exposes only migrate and serve. Add user-facing CLI weight add/list behavior or a dedicated CLI harness, then run the CLI-oriented agent eval variant and report it alongside the SDK and generated-client variants.

title: Investigate no-write agent eval failures
description: Final clean oh-5yr rerun after harness fixes shows no-write failures in both runnable variants. Production failed invalid-input by writing 2026-03-31 70.0 lb; generated-client failed ambiguous-short-date by writing 2026-03-29 152.2 lb and failed invalid-input by writing 2026-03-31 70.0 lb. Production passed ambiguous-short-date on the final clean run. Decide whether to strengthen skill validation guidance/runtime guardrails/adjust eval prompts/accept variance; see docs/agent-eval-results/oh-5yr-2026-04-16.md.

title: Expand non-weight health ingestion and write surfaces
description: The v1 contract currently ships weight writes plus read paths for blood pressure, medications, and labs. Follow-up work should add explicit ingestion or write paths for the remaining health domains while preserving the OpenAPI-first contract and generated clients.

title: Establish ongoing security operations
description: Move beyond one-time security setup once the project has meaningful runtime or release surfaces. This includes a recurring dependency review cadence, advisory workflow rehearsal, and threat modeling, abuse-case testing, or fuzzing for sensitive functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Maintainers have a documented recurring security review cadence and, where relevant, a deeper testing plan for high-risk project surfaces.

title: Raise maintainer isolation as automation grows
description: As repository automation expands, reduce maintainer and bot blast radius. This includes dedicated low-privilege bot identities, stronger first-time contributor approval rules if warranted, self-hosted runner policy, and stricter review enforcement once more than one maintainer can satisfy it.
acceptance_criteria: Maintainer and automation identities follow least privilege, untrusted PR execution policy is documented, and branch/review enforcement matches the actual maintainer model without making the repo unworkable.

value: Release hardening landed on branch codex/local-runtime-release-hardening; remaining trust-boundary follow-up is GitHub release environment protection setup tracked in oh-9s9, which still blocks oh-ad6.
